sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lake Genevieve not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lake Genevieve not listed; the §7.50(b)(152) Lake Tahoe tributary rule covers tributary streams upstream to the first lake, not standing lakes)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Lake Genevieve not listed)

sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

sources.title: Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Day use is not subject to fees nor limited by quota at any time of the year.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 224112, "Lake Genevieve", Lake, El Dorado County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":224112,"gaz_name":"Lake Genevieve","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13376281.5142, 4721238.1715) converts to lon -120.161181, lat 38.996974, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-120.1611717, 38.9969933) to within ~10m.

parameters.note: Lake Genevieve, an alpine lake in the Desolation Wilderness (El Dorado Co.) and the first lake on Meeks Creek above Meeks Bay, is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b). The §7.50(b)(152) Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation by its terms covers Tahoe tributary streams 'upstream to the first lake' (i.e., Meeks Creek up to Lake Genevieve) and the lake within 300 ft of tributary mouths — not the standing lake itself — so it does not reach Lake Genevieve. The lake therefore falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Lake Genevieve lies within the Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest / Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit). A wilderness permit is required to enter: a free day-use permit (self-issued at major trailheads, no fee and not quota-limited) and, for overnight trips, an overnight permit required year-round. Overnight use is governed by a zone-quota system in effect from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 each year; overnight permits and reservations are handled through Recreation.gov (permit #233261).

raw.day_use_permit: required; free; no fee; not quota-limited
raw.overnight_quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USDA Forest Service — Desolation Wilderness Permits (Eldorado National Forest)
regulations.humanSummary: In Desolation Wilderness: a free day-use permit and (for overnight trips) an overnight permit required year-round; overnight zone-quota season runs the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 (reserve via Recreation.gov)
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.
